brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is resampling-based distribution-free uncertainty quantification (block/residual/sieve/wild bootstrap plus adaptive conformal calibrators) whose goal is valid coverage of prediction intervals under temporal dependence, whereas Paper B's machinery is cosmological two-point clustering estimation (angular correlations, power-spectrum ratios P_gg/P_gK, LambdaCDM Halofit and HOD/NFW halo models) to infer galaxy bias, sigma_8 and halo masses. No role-for-role correspondence holds: bootstrap replicates, conformal residuals and coverage have no counterpart in power spectra, bias ratios or halo occupation fits, so the only overlap is the generic notion of 'estimating a statistic with error bars.'
